title: The Baal Cycle: Baal Against the Sea
description: The Canaanite creation myth: the storm god Baal defeats the sea god Yam and builds his palace on Mount Zaphon.
introduction: The Baal Cycle, recovered from clay tablets at Ugarit (modern Ras Shamra, Syria), is the most important Canaanite mythological text. It narrates the storm god Baal's battles against Yam (Sea) and Mot (Death), his enthronement as king of the gods, and the construction of his palace on Mount Zaphon. The cycle illuminates the religious background of the Hebrew Bible — many of its motifs appear transformed in the Psalms, Job, and the prophets.

translation.original_text: mt . ym . mt . ym . b'l . ymlk
translation.text: Sea is indeed dead! Baal shall be king!
I shall build a house of cedar and brick, a house of lapis lazuli and silver.
